text: RFID-Based Attendance Tracking System with Web Interface and Excel Integration (AttendEX)
The Project Study Presented to
The Faculty of South East Asian Institute of Technology
College of Information and Communication Technology
In Partial Fulfillment
of the Requirement for the subject
IT 412: Capstone Project and Research 2
By:
Celis, Summer Dyan
Elloren, Christine Joy P.
Lictawa, Mitch Azaira G.
May 2024
Abstract

“RFID-Based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with Web Interface and Excel Integration:” moderating system for recording and communicating between the moderator and officers, it is a crucial solution for concerns of SEAIT Supreme Student Council attendance record and performance management. With the help of the Agile approach, researchers will build up the RFID-Based Attendance Tracking System emphasizing on the capabilities including easy point of use, quick attendance record and to the moderator SMS notification functions. Extensive trials have proved the correctness, transparency, and sturdiness of the system with the users giving very positive feedback for the product and scoring it highly on the effectiveness of safety and security attributes. The system was successfully implemented by utilizing both RFID and SMS technology in the process, which resulted in efficient monitoring and management of attendance and logs of the officers, which provided supreme student council security and efficient information management.
Keywords: RFID-Based Attendance Tracking System, SMS notification, Agile approach, Managing and Recording Solutions

CHAPTER I
Rationale of the Study

The AttendEX system is an RFID-Based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with a Web Interface and Excel Integration is a software developed for timely and daily monitoring logbook information of the officers and committee in South East Asian Institute of Technology, Inc. (SEAIT). It will be a great advantage to the student officers, committee and school, for the reason that it will be providing a quicker and smarter way to monitor the regular attendance. It is designed to simplify attendance management for administrators and users, providing a more efficient and user-friendly solution. In educational institutions and various organizations, attendance tracking is a critical process. In our modern era, where digital tools and systems have the potential to revolutionize everyday processes, it is essential to optimize attendance management. The AttendEX system aims to achieve that objective. It has a variety of features that greatly improve the administrator's capacity to handle attendance easily. From the moment of registration, the system associates each user with an RFID card or tag, ensuring a smooth and efficient start. It enhances attendance accuracy, reducing the likelihood of errors. Moreover, the system exports attendance data into Excel, serving as a secondary database for the system. The AttendEX system provides SMS alerts to the administrator, ensuring timely awareness of attendance. Every time attendance is recorded, it automatically sends a notification through an SMS alert to the administrator. Additionally, it includes a peer assessment feedback feature, promoting interaction and feedback. Furthermore, it offers report generation and visualization capabilities, allowing administrators to analyze data and make informed decisions with ease.

The current process of the Supreme Student Council (SSC) is the official student governing body at the South East Asian Institute of Technology. The attendance of the officers and committee uses pen and paper when recording all the data of the student officers. Upon their arrival, and departure required to record their names, date, and time in a logbook to monitor attendance. Based on the statement of Mitch Azaira G. Lictawa, SSC Business Manager. The process of the officers and committee during events and daily duties is using the logbook that has 150 pages with a regular size of 7”x11” which is not appropriate considering that they needs more spaces for column of collection of data. According to the statement of the officers they are the ones who that will draw lines so that the information will be written in their perspective data column. After receiving the data all day, they will have to check and return the collected data and information to the moderator, Mr. Clar Don L. Evangelio, MEqEng.. This process extends to student organizations such as Silakbo, Peer Facilitators, ROTC, and other clubs willing to contribute, each of which must manually document their attendance, affiliation, date, and time in the logbook.

The problem encountered of manual attendance tracking system used by the Supreme Student Council (SSC) at the South East Asian Institute of Technology faces several significant challenges. (1) One prominent issue is the manual recording of names and times for both time-in and time-out, resulting in redundant data entries. This not only consumes valuable time but also creates opportunities for errors and erasures, making the logbook cluttered and unreliable. (2) Additionally, manual attendance methods often suffer from inaccuracies, especially during events, and lack precise timestamp information. When attendance records are manually transferred to Excel, it worsens these problems, introducing the potential for data entry errors and inconsistent records. (3) Slow communication of attendance information to moderators can lead to delays in decision-making and accountability issues during meetings and events. (4) Furthermore, the difficulty in assessing individual officer contributions hinders teamwork and overall effectiveness. (5) Nevertheless, the process of manually analyzing data for decision-making remains slow and error-prone, impacting the efficiency of meetings and events.

The benefits of having system, AttendEx, offers a range of practical features to address the ongoing challenges faced by the Supreme Student Council (SSC) at the South East Asian Institute of Technology. At its core, AttendEx simplifies the registration of officers and committee members through a user-friendly interface, ensuring that important details like names and unique student IDs are captured with ease. This system relies on RFID technology, associating each officer and committee member with RFID cards or tags to make attendance tracking effortless. Improved attendance accuracy and efficiency come from automatically recording attendance during duties, activities, and events, including significant occasions like Intramural, College Week, and Foundation Day. AttendEx captures attendance accurately, providing detailed date and time information for thorough tracking. To simplify data management, the system smoothly integrates attendance data with Excel, acting as a secondary database. Introducing SMS alerts customized for the moderator ensures that they receive real-time information, aiding swift decision-making and accountability during SSC meetings and events. Moreover, AttendEx includes a Peer Assessment Feedback feature, empowering SSC officers to evaluate their peers' contributions and performance, fostering a culture of teamwork and collaboration within the SSC. These peer assessments are accessible to the moderator for team assessment and continuous improvement. To top it all, AttendEx automatically creates reports and visualizations, making it simple for the moderator to analyze data and make informed decisions. To top it all, AttendEx is set to modernize attendance management within the SSC, making processes more efficient, improving accuracy, and creating a more cohesive and effective student governing body.

Conclusion, The system needs to be implemented to lessen the workload of the moderator, to monitor the attendance of the officers legitimately, and to prevent some Supreme Student Council from tampering with the lack of personnel during the events and activities.This will show the easiest way of performing their tasks that will create a whole new set of learning and performance processes in favor to the school, student body.
Objectives of the Study
General Objectives

The researcher aims to study and develop a  RFID-Based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with Web Interface and Excel Integration (AttendEX) for the Supreme Student Council (SSC) in South East Asian Institute of Technology, Inc.
Specific Objectives

To be able to develop a system that will simplify officer and committee registration, with an RFID card or tag that will provide an easy-to-use interface for capturing essential details, including names and unique student IDs.
To be able to develop a system that automatically records attendance by scanning RFID cards during duties, activities or events which are College Week, Foundation Day and other activities and events.
To be able to develop a system that will send SMS alerts specifically to the moderator regarding the attendance of officers and committees during duties, events, or activities.

To be able to develop a system that includes a Peer Evaluation Assessment Feedback, allowing SSC officers to provide evaluations and feedback on their peers' contributions and performance visible to the moderator for team assessment and improvement.
To be able to develop a system that will automatically generates attendance reports and evaluation visualizations, making it easy for the moderator to analyze data and make informed decisions.
Scope and Limitations of the Study
Scope of the Study

The study will focus on the development and implementation of RFID-Based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with Web Interface and Excel Integration. This study will be conducted and implemented at the Supreme Student Council Office of the South East Asian Institute of Technology.
The scope of the study is centered on RFID-Based Attendance with Web Interface and Excel Integration where the following are possible:
Register officers, and committees.
Explanation
RFID scanning attendance
Explanation
SMS alerts message and monitor attendance tracking.
Explanation
Peer assessment feedback of officer’s evaluation form.
Explanation
Generate attendance reports in excel and analysis visualizations.
Explanation
Limitation of the Study

The system is solely implemented within the SSC Office at South East Asian Institute of Technology, and its use is limited to the officers and committees of the Supreme Student Council. The system is incapable on identifying the awardees, producing any layout certificates, calculate the budgetary plan for events, and produce picture of the officers.
Significance of the Study
Our study is significant to the following group:
Organization
To provide a system that will accurately and reliably handle the attendance of SSC officers and committees at the South East Asian Institute of Technology, Inc.
SSC Moderators
The study's findings are crucial for SSC moderator, as they simplify attendance management, save time, and provide accurate data.
Researchers
The researcher gains a comprehensive understanding of the challenges faced by SSC moderators/officers/committees in tracking attendance and how technology can address them.
Future Researchers

Future researchers can build upon the findings of this study; expanding the knowledge base and making further advancements in RFID-Based Attendance Tracking System with Web Interface and Excel Integration.

Definition of terms

The definition of terms of RFID-Based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with Web Interface and Excel Integration are statements expressing the essential nature of the study that enables a common understanding of key terms within the system.

AttendEX - is a cutting-edge attendance management system that combines innovation and efficiency to revolutionize the way attendance is handled. The 'E' in 'AttendEx' represents seamless Excel integration, allowing for effortless data management and analysis. The 'X' signifies the system's commitment to harnessing the power of technology to provide an innovative and user-friendly solution.
Attendance Tracking - is a method used to monitor officers duty schedule and performance.
Committees - are group of individuals, typically appointed or elected with a specific task or purpose within an organization, including Peer Facilitator, Silakbo, and etc.

Excel Integration - the process of integrating attendance data of officers into a Microsoft Excel spreadsheet. This integration allows for easy manipulation, analysis, and visualization of attendance data.
Moderator - is an individual appointed or selected to oversee and facilitate discussions, meetings, or activities within a group, organization, or event.

Peer Evaluation - is a process in which officers within an organization evaluate each other's performance. This method is used to assess the strengths and weaknesses of each officer, identify areas for improvement, and provide feedback to help them grow professionally.

RFID-Based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation with Web Interface and Excel Integration - a system that will manage attendance data and records attendance by scanning RFID card or tag and can export attendance data in Excel.

RFID - Radio-Frequency Identification (RFID) is a technology that uses wireless communication to identify and track objects, people, or animals. It consists of RFID tags or labels, and RFID readers or scanners that capture this data.
SSC - Supreme Student Council (SSC) refers to the official student governing body at South East Asian Institute of Technology.
SMS - refers to a notification system that sends a text message to a designated recipient which id the SSC Moderator when an officer has arrived at or left a designated location.
CHAPTER II
Review of Related Literature and Studies

This chapter includes ideas, finished thesis, generalization or conclusion, methodologies and others. Those that were included in this chapter helps in familiarizing information that are relevant and similar to the present study.
Review of Related Literature
Foreign Literature
A New Model of the Student Attendance Monitoring System Using RFID Technology

According to Ula et al., (2021), today, the development of information technology has been widely applied to make work easier, faster and more streamlined. The timekeeping system still in place is of the paper-based, manual timekeeping precession type and is inefficient in terms of time, compiling data on the existence and accuracy or authenticity of the data presented. Leaving a signature as proof of the employee's presence is still commonly done. This research aims to provide a new model for promoting attendance, a timekeeping system using RFID technology. RFID will use sensors to read data. The use of RFID is intended to make it easier for teachers and students to track course participation. On RFID tags, radio frequencies are attached to the reader; the system will automatically send student data to the database. So, students can do it quickly and data can also be unified quickly and completely. In addition, students and lecturers are also motivated to arrive early because the system can store and display live data.
Attendance System Design And Implementation Based On Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) And Arduino

According to Alrikabi et al., (2018), In the modern era, there is an increasing need for safety systems to be integrated into automatic identification technology programs. The objective of this article is to design and implement an access control system to create one of the most popular automatic identifications using a radio frequency identification (RFID) tool controlled by Arduino technology. RFID applications are very efficient and fast and can identify any physical object such as an automatic attendance recording system. The system has been implemented in this article and includes RFID, Arduino Mega, SD card module, keypad, LED and 7 segments. To control the accompanying students inside the classroom or laboratory takes a lot of time. Therefore, this article is designed to overcome these problems using the design and implementation of a connected RFID-ARDUINO system in a classroom or web-based laboratory timekeeping system. The referral system will help instructors register and get students enrolled without delay. In short, with the proposed system, all the obstacles and errors previously encountered by researchers have been resolved. By placing this low-cost device in every classroom in our department and creating a card for everyone like an RFID card to give each person their card to be used for registration without any hassle.
IoT-School Attendance System Using RFID Technology

According to Mrabet & Moussa (2020), today, the world has witnessed tremendous changes that have increased the pace of life. For their part, students expect a simple and engaging learning experience that takes into account this fast pace and helps them solve stereotypical learning problems. The analyzes performed in this article show that recording class attendance reduces teaching-learning time. So, the article is talking about researching an effective way to record school attendance without hindering learning. This article presents and discusses the smart attendance monitoring system in smart classroom as a means to reduce the dropout rate, using connected objects and RFID technology. This system can facilitate the recording of students' attendance via the Internet of things (IoT) at presential while transmitting attendance record to the administration. At the same time, parents can keep track of their children's attendance to classes through a notification they receive via Email or SMS. In addition, the system automatically sends missed lessons to absent students. The implemented system offers a lot of benefits over traditional system. In traditional method of attendance system, the recording of attendance in classrooms is always done at the expense of the learning time. The IoT-Attendance system help teachers allocate more time to students learning rather than spend their time on traditional recording. The IoT-Attendance evaluate shows that the model is effective and has a potential impact to improve student performance and strengthen collaboration between parents and school to monitor student attendance in the classroom. Moreover, when students miss class, missed lessons can be automatically sent to absent students, which increase learning opportunities for all students and get good marks in their exams. The performance of student is also positively affected by the use of the IoT-Attendance system.
Local Literature
RFID Based Attendance System

According to Joshi et al., (2021), RFID is an acronym for “radio-frequency identification” RFID technology is an automatic wireless identification system that works by the help of two components: a card and a reader. Using the RFID technology, the conventional system of taking attendance can be completely transformed to be more in line with strides in digitalization. This system will help the authorities manage the attendance system in a more methodical, efficient and time saving manner. The proposed system has been implemented through a prototype that has proved the effectiveness of the concept in easing the logistics of taking attendance as a result of the automation due to the use of the RFID technology. The design of the system is simple, cost effective and agile making it a good candidate for commercial and academic purposes. The successful design and implementation of an automated attendance system based on RFID technology has been successfully presented through the means of this paper. The system developed provides an accurate, simple and cost-effective means to register the attendance in educational institutions and effectively shifts the paradigm towards a digital and contactless environment. Moreover, owing to the compactness and the portability of the system, it makes it all the more conducive to be deployed as and when required.
Student Attendance System Using RFID

According to Nivetha et al., (2020), in an educational system, attendance is considered as an important criterion to assess the performance of the student. A student is expected to perform well if he is regular to college. The traditional method of marking the student’s attendance is by calling the student one by one. This method consumes more time. In this technological era, everything is automized. In this paper we propose a system to monitor and record the attendance of the system using RFID and Arduino. Every student will be given unique RFID. When the students scan RFID card in front of RFID card reader, the attendance is automatically marked by reading the RFID tag in the card. It is then stored in Microsoft Excel file. Utilizing RFID technology for marking attendance is extremely well effective and saves the time of both the teacher and the student.
Web-Based Attendance Monitoring System

According to Amora et al., (2020), wireless attendance monitoring system is a system developed to track student attendance during school days. This project features student’s ID card tapping in the terminal and displays student profile in the portal. An area for announcements is also added in the portal aside from sending it via SMS. More so, it also features an admin panel, including a dashboard that displays reports in graphical view for easy system monitoring as well as it has full wireless computing technology. The software and technologies used in this project are Raspberry Pi, Database Server, RFID Reader, Arduino NMCU, Python, PHP, MySQL, Bootstrap, and CSS. A wireless web-based attendance monitoring system was developed using the scrum framework of agile methodology. Aside from its wireless feature which is not implemented in existing systems, the system also has prominent features like SMS notification, reports management and announcement management. This could help not only in monitoring the attendance of students but also in generating reports. Hence, this system would likely support academic institutions which would need to monitor students’ attendance daily.
Review of Related Studies
Foreign Studies
Smart Attendance Monitoring System using IoT and RFID

According to Bharathy et al., ( 2021), Maintaining track of students' attendance in a school is a difficult endeavor. An ongoing challenge is managing attendance by hand. The project's goal is to create an intelligent attendance system that effectively and automatically tracks students' attendance in a facility. RFID readers and an Arduino Uno microcontroller are used in the development of the entire system. Students' ID cards can be equipped with individual RFID tags. Additionally, Wi-Fi communication modules are used to enable convenient communication contingent on network availability. A student database has to be established. Parents' mobile phones receive alerts regarding their child's attendance status via a GSM module. A GPS module is utilized to determine the student's current location. The system will reduce a lot of manual work of teachers and administrators of any institution. The proposed work comprises of two most popular trend in technology research; IoT and RFID.
Attendance and Information System using RFID and Web-Based Application for Academic Sector

According to Rjeib et al., (2018), recently, student attendance is considered one of the important factors or issues that reflect the academic results and performance of any university compared to traditional methods that are time-consuming and ineffective. Fruit. Various automatic identification technologies are becoming more popular, such as radio frequency identification (RFID). Extensive research and several applications were carried out to make the most of this technology and raised some concerns. RFID is a wireless technology used for the purpose of identifying and tracking an object through radio waves to transmit data from an electronic tag, called an RFID tag or tag to send data to an RFID reader. The present study focuses on proposing an RFID-based attendance management system “AMS”; at the same time, it is an information service system serving the academic field using RFID technology in addition to programmable logic circuits (such as Arduino) and web applications. The proposed system aims to manage student attendance and also provide a means to track absent students. Support information services include student grades, daily timetables, class hours and class numbers as well as other student-related guidance provided by the department's faculty staff. Based on the results, the proposed information and timekeeping system is fast, reduces recording effort, and consumes no energy. Furthermore, students studying the proposed RFID-based systems are also analyzed and critiqued regarding the system features and key conclusions. Future directions for future researchers are targeted and identified.
The Proposed Implementation of RFID based Attendance System

According to Qureshi (2020), recent trends in information and communication technology (ICT) include a number of smartphone applications in various educational and industrial sectors in recent years. This research aims to solve one of the promising problems of the education field: smart attendance using radio frequency identification (RFID) system. The current attendance system at King Abdul-Aziz University (KAU) in Saudi Arabia partly solves the attendance problem. There are a number of problems in the current attendance system such as wastage of time, risk of errors, truancy issues, lack of communication with parents/guardians and inefficiencies due to attendance as well as grades craft name. The proposed RFID-based time attendance system provides robust, secure, and automated time attendance capabilities. The proposed system will use modern technology and support institutions and parents to solve most of the problems of the existing school attendance system. There are several other benefits of RFID-based systems such as web and mobile interfaces, daily absence reporting, automatic SMS alerts to parents/guardians, reduced administrative work, improved School attendance rate, economy and high efficiency. The case study method will be used as the research model. The proposed system was developed and tested at KAU Saudi Arabia. The proposed system will have both web and mobile interfaces. The web interface will need the Internet to access the proposed system, and the mobile interface will use the Android platform for testing scenarios. Users will access the system to generate personalized reports to review student status for a specific course. It is expected that the proposed system will significantly improve the student monitoring mechanism, thereby facilitating parents and teachers to make appropriate decisions.
Local Studies
iNotify&Monitor System with Data Analytics using RFID and Mobile Broadband Technology

According to Santander (2018), Effective reporting and monitoring of student attendance is one of the important developments that any higher education institution must have. This research mainly focuses on the design and development of the iNotify&Monitor system with the ability to analyze data using RFID and mobile broadband technology. This is a 3-in-1 software that helps Jose Rizal Memorial State Campus-Katipunan (JRMSU-K) improve its processes by notifying students whenever there are important announcements such as course cancellations, delays, activity schedule. or even alert in an emergency for student safety, monitor student attendance, and review registration and attendance records to make better decisions. The tools used to develop this software are Microsoft Visual Studio C#, PHP and MySQL. Based on the system's evaluation, it was rated as “very effective.” Subsequently, we strongly encourage the immediate use of this system as it is expected to improve student management at the university. In summary, after extensive analysis and design, the iNotify&Monitor system at the JRMSU-K campus was successfully developed. It was developed not only to improve current methods of reporting and tracking student attendance, but also to minimize or eliminate problems encountered when using current systems. During the design and development of this system, all functional and non-functional requirements specified by the end user are considered and applied. Students, SSG staff and IT professionals trust and believe that this system is very effective. Therefore, it is highly recommended that the system should be immediately implemented in order to instantly notify students on important announcements and conveniently and effectively monitor students' attendance. Even while the study focused on the JRMSU-K Campus in the development of the system, the structure and the processes presented in the study is generic enough for any higher education institution to pattern the development of its respective notification and student attendance monitoring system.
In-Classroom Faculty Attendance Monitoring System based on Ultra High Frequency (UHF) Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) with Captured Image Cross-verification Mechanism

According to Calo et al., (2021), teacher attendance at school is monitored based on the Daily Time Record (DTR), which records teachers' daily entry and exit times on and off school premises using a variety of technologies and methods. However, research on teacher supervision is rare. Attendance in the classroom itself. Therefore, this study aims to develop a teacher attendance monitoring system based on ultra-high frequency (UHF) radio frequency identification (RFID) with a mechanism for cross-verification of captured images. Method: The system includes two modules, which are the Teacher's Time Attendance Module and the Time Attendance Monitoring Web Application Module. The teacher presence module includes a UHF-RFID reader and a camera module that communicates with a Raspberry Pi card. Passive labels are integrated into the teacher's identification card so that the UHF-RFID reader can uniquely identify them when they are there within a 2-meter reading distance. Meanwhile, the attendance monitoring web application module is developed using a PHP framework connected to a MySQL database. It aggregates attendance data collected from the Teacher Attendance Module for tracking purposes, which both monitoring staff and teachers can access via a web browser. Result: Based on the evaluation results, the proposed system received positive feedback from end users regarding functionality and usability, and was found to be more beneficial than existing attendance monitoring systems. Novelty: This system provides a non-intrusive way to verify teacher attendance using UHF-RFID technology that has a longer read range than other types of RFID used in previous studies. Additionally, unlike existing solutions, the system checks attendance multiple times, not just at entry and exit, and then calculates the teacher's total attendance time during a class session. More importantly, the system can prevent fraudulent activities through the camera module.
IT Center Attendance Monitoring System using RFID

According to Dultiao & Duldulao (2019), the development of the student attendance system at the university's computer center is based on radio frequency identification (RFID) technology. The development of technology to improve the delivery of educational services has become widespread, but some educational institutions do not adopt it and want to live by tradition. The attendance tracking system in some universities and high schools is still done manually. Therefore, this study proposes an attendance monitoring system using RFID to manage student attendance records and ensure easy attendance tracking in the computer center. It is expected that after implementing the system, teachers will benefit from the ability to effectively monitor student attendance; Therefore, the quality of teaching and student learning outcomes can be improved.
CHAPTER III
Research Methodology and Design

This chapter presents theories associated in obtaining necessary to be used in this study, software methodology, research design and system development phases which include the strategy, design, development, testing, deployment and maintenance.
Environment
The study took place at Supreme Student Council Office at South East Asian Institute of Technology in National Highway, Crossing Rubber, Tupi, 9505, South Cotabato, Philippines.
Software Engineering Methodology

The Agile Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) was used in the software development for the completion of AttendEX System as it is the combination of both iterative and incremental process. The said model comprises six (6) phases, which include;
Figure 3.1:
Agile Model has six phases for AttendEX or RFID-Based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with Web Interface and Excel Integration that illustrates the System Development Process.
System Development Phases
Strategy

Strategy for S.O.1 (To be able to develop a system that will simplify officer and committee registration, with an RFID card or tag that will provide an easy-to-use interface for capturing essential details, including names and unique student IDs)
To simplify officer and committee registration, the researchers strategized to create an easy-to-use registration system for capturing essential details, including names and unique student IDs.

Strategy for S.O.2 (To be able to develop a system that enhances attendance accuracy and efficiency, the system automatically records attendance by scanning RFID cards during duties, activities or events which are College Week, Foundation Day and other activities and events.)
To enhance attendance accuracy and efficiency, the researchers have strategized to associate each officer and committee with an RFID card for the system to scan, ensuring a smooth process.
Strategy for S.O.3 (To be able to develop a system that will send SMS alerts specifically to the moderator regarding the attendance of officers and committees during duties, events, or activities.)

To ensure that the moderator stays informed in real-time about the attendance, the researchers have strategized to include an SMS alert feature in the system, which will send notifications to the moderator every time an attendance is recorded.

Strategy for S.O.4 (To be able to develop a system that includes a Peer Evaluation Assessment Feedback feature, allowing SSC officers to provide evaluations and feedback on their peers' contributions and performance, promoting teamwork and collaboration within the SSC. The submitted peer assessments are visible to the moderator for team assessment and improvement.)
To allow SSC officers to provide evaluations and feed back on their peer’s contributions and performance. The researchers have strategized to include a Peer Assessment Feedback feature in the system.

Strategy for S.O.5 (To be able to develop a system that will automatically generates attendance reports and evaluation visualizations, making it easy for the moderator to analyze data and make informed decisions.)
To assist the moderator in data analysis, the researchers have strategized to incorporate report generation and visualization feature into the system.
Design

Design for S.O.1 (To be able to develop a system that will simplify officer and committee registration, with an RFID card or tag that will provide an easy-to-use interface for capturing essential details, including names and unique student IDs.)

In the design phase, the researchers conceptualize an easy-to-use registration system that captures essential details, including names and unique student IDs, to simplify officer and committee registration.

Design for S.O.2 (To be able to develop a system that enhances attendance accuracy and efficiency, the system automatically records attendance by scanning RFID cards during duties, activities or events which are College Week, Foundation Day and other activities and events.)

In the design phase, the researchers plan to incorporate RFID card integration to ensure a smooth process for associating each officer and committee with an RFID card or tag, enhancing attendance tracking.

Design for S.O.3 (To be able to develop a system that enhances attendance accuracy and efficiency, the system automatically records attendance by scanning RFID cards during duties, activities or events which are College Week, Foundation Day and other activities and events.)

In the design phase, the researchers strategized to design an SMS alert feature in the system. This feature is intended to send real-time notifications to the moderator every time an attendance is recorded, aiding in decision-making and accountability during meetings and events.

Design for S.O.4 (To be able to develop a system that includes a Peer Evaluation Assessment Feedback feature, allowing SSC officers to provide evaluations and feedback on their peers' contributions and performance, promoting teamwork and collaboration within the SSC. The submitted peer assessments are visible to the moderator for team assessment and improvement.)

Design for S.O.5 (To be able to develop a system that will automatically generates attendance reports and evaluation visualizations, making it easy for the moderator to analyze data and make informed decisions.)

In the design phase, the researchers aim to design a system that automatically generates reports and visualizations. This design will make it easy for the moderator to analyze data and make informed decisions.
Development

Development for S.O.1 (To be able to develop a system that will simplify officer and committee registration, with an RFID card or tag that will provide an easy-to-use interface for capturing essential details, including names and unique student IDs.)

In the development phase, the researchers work on creating an easy-to-use registration system that captures essential details, including names and unique student IDs, to simplify officer and committee registration.

Development for S.O.2 (To be able to develop a system that enhances attendance accuracy and efficiency, the system automatically records attendance by scanning RFID cards during duties, activities or events which are College Week, Foundation Day and other activities and events.)

Development for S.O.3 (To be able to develop a system that will send SMS alerts specifically to the moderator regarding the attendance of officers and committees during duties, events, or activities.)

Testing

Testing for S.O.1 (To be able to develop a system that will simplify officer and committee registration, with an RFID card or tag that will provide an easy-to-use interface for capturing essential details, including names and unique student IDs.)

In the testing phase, the researchers evaluate the registration system's functionality, ensuring it simplifies officer and committee registration. They check if it effectively captures essential details, including names and unique student IDs.

Testing for S.O.2 (To be able to develop a system that enhances attendance accuracy and efficiency, the system automatically records attendance by scanning RFID cards during duties, activities or events which are College Week, Foundation Day and other activities and events.)

Testing for S.O.3 (To be able to develop a system that will send SMS alerts specifically to the moderator regarding the attendance of officers and committees during duties, events, or activities.)

Testing for S.O.4 (To be able to develop a system that includes a Peer Evaluation Assessment Feedback feature, allowing SSC officers to provide evaluations and feedback on their peers' contributions and performance, promoting teamwork and collaboration within the SSC. The submitted peer assessments are visible to the moderator for team assessment and improvement.)

They ensure that SSC officers can provide evaluations and feedback on their peers' contributions and performance, promoting teamwork and collaboration. They also check that submitted peer assessments are visible to the moderator for team assessment and improvement.

Testing for S.O.5 (To be able to develop a system that will automatically generates attendance reports and evaluation visualizations, making it easy for the moderator to analyze data and make informed decisions.)

Deployment

Deployment for S.O.1 (To be able to develop a system that will simplify officer and committee registration, with an RFID card or tag that will provide an easy-to-use interface for capturing essential details, including names and unique student IDs.)

In the deployment phase, the researchers roll out the registration system to the intended users, ensuring that it simplifies officer and committee registration by providing an easy-to-use interface for capturing essential details, including names and unique student IDs.

Deployment for S.O.2 (To be able to develop a system that enhances attendance accuracy and efficiency, the system automatically records attendance by scanning RFID cards during duties, activities or events which are College Week, Foundation Day and other activities and events.)

In the deployment phase, the researchers implement the RFID card integration and attendance tracking features, making them available to users. This deployment ensures a smooth process for associating each officer and committee with RFID cards or tags, enhancing attendance tracking.

Deployment for S.O.3 (To be able to develop a system that will send SMS alerts specifically to the moderator regarding the attendance of officers and committees during duties, events, or activities. )

In the deployment phase, the SMS alert feature is activated and accessible to users. This deployment ensures that the moderator receives real-time notifications every time an attendance is recorded, aiding in decision-making and accountability during meetings and events.

Deployment for S.O.4 (To be able to develop a system that includes a Peer Evaluation Assessment Feedback feature, allowing SSC officers to provide evaluations and feedback on their peers' contributions and performance, promoting teamwork and collaboration within the SSC. The submitted peer assessments are visible to the moderator for team assessment and improvement.)

In the deployment phase, the Peer Assessment Feedback feature is made available to SSC officers. This deployment allows SSC officers to provide evaluations and feedback on their peers' contributions and performance, promoting teamwork and collaboration. It also makes submitted peer assessments visible to the moderator for team assessment and improvement.

Deployment for S.O.5 (To be able to develop a system that will automatically generates attendance reports and evaluation visualizations, making it easy for the moderator to analyze data and make informed decisions.)

In the deployment phase, the researchers make the report generation and visualization features accessible to the moderator. This deployment ensures that the system automatically generates reports and visualizations, making it easy for the moderator to analyze data and make informed decisions.
Maintenance

Maintenance for S.O.1 (To be able to develop a system that will simplify officer and committee registration, with an RFID card or tag that will provide an easy-to-use interface for capturing essential details, including names and unique student IDs.)

In the maintenance phase, the researchers ensure the ongoing functionality and effectiveness of the registration system. They continue to provide support and updates to maintain its ability to simplify officer and committee registration, including capturing essential details.

Maintenance for S.O.2 (To be able to develop a system that enhances attendance accuracy and efficiency, the system automatically records attendance by scanning RFID cards during duties, activities or events which are College Week, Foundation Day and other activities and events.)

In the maintenance phase, the researchers the researchers continue to monitor and support the RFID card integration and attendance tracking features. Regular updates and maintenance are performed to ensure a smooth process for associating each officer and committee with RFID cards or tags, enhancing attendance tracking.

Maintenance for S.O.3 (To be able to develop a system that will send SMS alerts specifically to the moderator regarding the attendance of officers and committees during duties, events, or activities.)

In the maintenance phase, the automated attendance recording system is regularly checked and updated. This ongoing maintenance ensures the system continues to enhance attendance accuracy and efficiency by automatically recording attendance during duties, activities, and events using RFID cards.

Maintenance for S.O.4 (To be able to develop a system that includes a Peer Evaluation Assessment Feedback feature, allowing SSC officers to provide evaluations and feedback on their peers' contributions and performance, promoting teamwork and collaboration within the SSC. The submitted peer assessments are visible to the moderator for team assessment and improvement.)

In the maintenance phase, the researchers maintain the Peer Assessment Feedback feature. Ongoing support and updates are provided to ensure SSC officers can continue to provide evaluations and feedback on their peers' contributions and performance. The feature's ongoing maintenance promotes teamwork and collaboration within the SSC and maintains visibility of submitted peer assessments for team assessment and improvement.

Maintenance for S.O.5 (To be able to develop a system that will automatically generates attendance reports and evaluation visualizations, making it easy for the moderator to analyze data and make informed decisions.)

In the maintenance phase, ongoing support and updates are provided to maintain the system's ability to automatically generate reports and visualizations. This ensures that the moderator can continue to analyze data and make informed decisions with ease.

Network Design

Network design is the integration of different network devices to achieve end-to-end communication between network hosts, and to identify what type of topology is going to be used RFID-Based Attendance with Web Interface and Excel Integration.
Network Model

The network model that will be using is star topology model, In a star topology, the messages and each component is directly connected to the central hub that acts as a server and the connecting nodes as a client.
Figure 3.41:
Software Network Model of RFID-based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with Web Interface and Excel Integration (AttendEX).
Figure 3.42:
Hardware Network Model of RFID-based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with Web Interface and Excel Integration (AttendEX).
Network Topology

Star topology, a star network topology is characterized by a central hub or server that serves as the focal point of the network. Devices, such as RFID scanners, GPRS shields, PCs with user interfaces, and Wi-Fi access points, connect directly to the central hub. Data communication is efficiently managed through the hub, ensuring centralized control and reliability. This topology allows for easy scalability and isolates device issues, minimizing network disruptions.
Figure 3.43: Star Networking Topology of RFID-based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with Web Interface and Excel Integration (AttendEX).
List of Modules
Manage Officers

This module provide the administrator a process of registering SSC officers and committee members the system provide a key feature that is user-friendly interface for capturing essential user details, including names and unique student IDs.
Manage Attendance

This module provide the administrator RFID attendance optimizes attendance tracking for SSC officers and committees  the system provide a key feature that are association of RFID cards or tags with SSC officers and committee members, automatic attendance recording through RFID card scanning and provide a SMS alerts sent to the moderator in real-time regarding attendance during duties, events, or activities.
Manage Evaluation

This module provide administrator an evaluation for the fosters teamwork and collaboration within the Supreme Student Council, this will provide SSC moderator a visualized evaluations and feedback of the team assessment.
Manage Report

This module provide the administrator generated reports and visual data representations for data analysis. The system provide a key feature that will  provide automatic report and visualization generation, facilitating data analysis and decision-making.
Attendance
This module provide the officers and committee a fast and accurate attendance by scanning of RFID that will automatically store attendance data time in and out during duties, events, and activities.
Evaluation
This module provide the officers an evaluations and feedbacks of their peer which are visible to the moderator for team assessment and improvement.
Testing/ Quality Assurance Phase
Unit Testing

During this phase, the project researcher are testing every function and codes entered match each feature of the system, Additionally, they verify that the data in the database and the system are identical. Researchers implement unit testing for RFID-Based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with a Web Interface and Excel Integration to make every component operate as intended. This method enhances the system's maintainability and dependability by assisting in the early detection and correction of faults. For such a system, the following is an organized method for carrying out unit tests:

Accessibility: Allow users to assess the main features of the software modules to see if they are functioning properly. Additionally, it enabled them to determine whether it meshes well with the system's interface design.

Accuracy: Provide users with the ability to assess the speed and accuracy of data initiated and encoded, as well as forms and outcomes that demonstrate how well all information provided by the software program conforms to the standard format required by the software application. Examine test results to find any issues. Examine and resolve the codebase's underlying problems. To verify the fixes, run the tests again.

Functionality: Provide users with the ability to assess how the system applies its modules' functions. describing in detail whether each goal was reached and completed correctly. Continue to update and add additional tests to cover the changes as development moves forward and new features are added.
Integration Testing

The system modules that are logically integrated and tested application projects are being tested by the researchers. focuses on verifying that the system's various modules or components interact as intended by testing their interactions. The importance of this kind of testing lies in its ability to find problems that unit testing could miss, like data flow issues, mismatched interfaces, and integration concerns.
Figure 3.44:
Integration Testing figure of RFID-based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with Web Interface and Excel Integration (AttendEX).
Integration Testing: Bottom-Up Integration Approach

Evaluation Methodology

Evaluation of the system is a critical part of development. This will be evaluated based on how well the system functions, meets its objectives, and delights its users. The study assessed the system's usability, accessibility, and acceptability using the descriptive-development research methodology.
Respondent of the Study
The respondent of the study was composed of 2 possible users the moderator and officer
This study used a checklist and a rating scale questionnaire to gather the needed data and the responses to evaluate the system.

The checklist was used to evaluate the functionality contains a checklist form. included in the system, and for the accuracy, and accessibility contains the list of all system objectives and deliverables that the researchers want the respondents to check and evaluate. The frequency counts and the percentage will be computed.
Weighted Mean. This tool was used to provide answer to the questions.
The formula is:
WM
Where:
WM = weighted mean
X = number of respondents
Wi = Weight given to each respondent
N = number of cases
To check the functionality, accessibility, acceptability, and accuracy of the system, a 5-point Likert Scale is shown in the table. The mean was computed and interpreted in the table below.
Table 3.7:
Interpretative Scale used to interpret the Weighted Mean

Human Resource Specification

Admin- must have the basic knowledge about technology such as opening and managing documents, typing, scanning or printing. He/She must also know basic how to use computing devices, must know basic file manage techniques, how to explore applications, and must know the background of this system including the attendance process using RFID card and reader to be used.
Employee- must be familiar with technologies such as RFID cards, a reader that scans for attendance, and desktop computers or laptops.
Risk Management Plan/ Framework
Risk Management Process
Figure 3.48: Risk Management Process Cycle of RFID-Based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with Web Interface and Excel Integration (AttendEX).
STEP 1: Risk Identification

During Risk identification, the project team will have an obligation to recognize and categorize risk sources. The project team and developers are responsible for detecting and troubleshooting any risks, including faults or possible problems, that might impact the system's structure and output. The hacker, who is part of the development team, will attempt to take control of the system to identify particular spots of vulnerability.
Step 3: Risk Prioritization

Prioritizing risks is the process of figuring out which ones require your immediate attention. This ought to be determined by the possibility of risk and the potential consequences. Prioritizing risks can be accomplished by comparing them to the project and determining which are more likely to materialize and which will have a greater impact.
Step 4: Risk Treatment an Response

The risk response planning lessen or eliminate project dangers as well as how to maximize their impact. The goal of project managers should be to get rid of threats before they happen. In addition, the project manager has to raise the probability and importance of opportunities while lowering the probability and impact of risk.
Step 5: Risk Monitoring and Evaluation

In a risk evaluation, estimated threats are compared to per-established risk criteria inside the organization. Risk factors may include related expenses and advantages, regulatory obligations, and system failures.
User-Guide
The Admin/User will be guided through the RFID-Based Attendance Tracking System and Evaluation with Web-Interface and Excel Integration.
1.0 System Login - The Admin will be required to enter his/her given username and password.
1.1 Officer Management - The Admin will be able to register officer’s RFID-cards and information such as: Personal Information, Position, course, and School ID number.
1.2 Officer Attendance - The Admin will be able to view the attendance and click the export button to export the data into Excel.
1.3 Evaluation - The Admin will review the the evaluations by officers to monitor their performance.
1.4 Reports - The Admin will see a bar graph showing the Officer’s performance over the month.
1.5 Dashboard - The dashboard will display the total officers and provides real-time total count of officers who attended on the current date.
1.6 The Attendance Frame will be connected to RFID-Card reader that will be used by officers to tap their RFID cards when logging in and logging out.
1.7 Evaluation Form - The user/Officer will be able to evaluate their co-officers and submit the form.
Installation Guide
Here are the following steps in installing AttendEx to your desktop/Laptop devices:
The installation process has 3 stages:
1. Installation of Required Software
2. Database Installation
3. System Installation
Install XAMPP for Windows:
Visit the official XAMPP website: https://www.apachefriends.org/download.html
Download the XAMPP installer for Windows.
Run the installer and follow the on-screen instructions to complete the installation.
2.Start Apache and MySQL:
Open the XAMPP Control Panel after installation.
Start the Apache server by clicking the "Start" button next to "Apache."
Start the MySQL server by clicking the "Start" button next to "MySQL."
3.Access MySQL Admin Panel:
Open your web browser.
Go to http://localhost/phpmyadmin/ to access the MySQL administration panel or click the “Admin” next to “Start” button.
Obtain AttendEx Zip File:
Obtain the AttendEx application zip files containing the system files.
Extract AttendEx Files:
Extract the system files to a directory within the XAMPP htdocs folder (typically located at C:\xampp\htdocs\).
Access AttendEx:
Run AttendEx by accessing it through your web browser.
The link to access the system: http://localhost/Attendex/signin.html
CHAPTER IV
Summary

The study found that AttendEx, RFID-Based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with Web Interface and Excel Integration created for the Supreme Student Council of South East Asian Institute of Technology (SEAIT), efficiently record the student body attendance and evaluation. AttendEx improves communication and performance capabilities by including user-friendly features like automated attendance, peer assistment evaluation, and SMS alerting systems for the moderator. Testing verifies AttendEx’s functioning, accuracy, and acceptance, with users strongly agreeing that it improves accuracy and security. The approach successfully addresses issues such as inefficient communication during an event, difficulty in monitoring attendance, performance rating, and obstacles encountered in decision making. AttendEx emerges as a dependable solution for the different monitoring needs of SEAIT Supreme Student Council, creating confidence among student body and fighting disinformation among the organizations.
Conclusion

In conclusion, “AttendEx, RFID-Based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with Web Interface and Excel Integration" is an important step in improving the accuracy and efficiency of the Supreme Student Council of South East Asian Institute of Technology (SEAIT). The research effectively identifies and tackles significant issues with attendance communication, performance, and student body obstacles at events. AttendEx is built using the Agile technique, with straightforward features and thorough testing to assure operation, accuracy, and acceptance. The findings highlight AttendEx's success in reducing inconsistent records and inaccuracy concerns, building trust with organizations, and countering misinformation and misunderstanding in the student body. AttendEx emerges as a dependable solution, offering SSC SEAIT with easily accessible and real-time records, ensuring an accountable and more efficient environment. Moving forward, the deployment and ongoing refinement of AttendEx provide prospective options for improving SSC managing attendance and performance at SEAIT and other organizational institutions confronting comparable difficulties.
Recommendation

The “AttendEx, RFID-Based Attendance Tracking and Evaluation System with Web Interface and Excel Integration” offers a transformative solution to streamline attendance monitoring and recording processes at the Supreme Student Council. Several recommendations are put forth to maximize its potential impact and ensure its seamless integration into daily operations. As a recommendation, AttendEx should make further improvements, such as optimizing the system to handle many simultaneous users and RFID scans by implementing load balancing, implementing algorithms to detect anomalies in attendance data, and integrating an advanced offline recording of attendance and evaluation system for accurate demand forecasting and resource allocation. Implementing these recommendations will elevate user satisfaction and position itself for sustained success and growth.
